name: codeweb
tagline: The living map of your codebase.
elevator: codeweb dissects a repository to its atomic parts — functions, classes, methods — wires them into a living call/import graph, tags each node's domain, and surfaces cross-domain overlap. It serves that one graph two ways: a self-contained interactive HTML map for humans, and 20 deterministic query tools over MCP for coding agents, so an agent can check what already exists and what an edit breaks before it writes a line.
philosophy: Deterministic — no LLM in the analysis loop; the same repo always yields the same graph.
Zero runtime dependencies — the whole pipeline runs on an empty node_modules.
Read-only by default — codeweb never executes the code it maps.
Evidence over guesswork — every finding is body-confirmed and pre-flighted against a regression gate.

modes.title: Map your own codebase
modes.desc: See the real dependency web, find where logic is duplicated, and get an ordered, cycle-safe plan to consolidate it.

modes.title: Review before you adopt
modes.desc: Clone a third-party repo read-only, fully map it, and get an adoption review before you commit to depending on it. codeweb never runs the target.

tools.name: codeweb_map
tools.desc: Build (or rebuild) the graph for a repo — the deterministic pipeline over MCP, so a session never dead-ends on "no graph found".

tools.name: codeweb_explain
tools.desc: One ~1KB card: identity, contract, dependents, blast radius, findings — "tell me about X" in a single call.

tools.name: codeweb_callers
tools.desc: Direct callers of a symbol (call-edge in-neighbors).

tools.name: codeweb_callees
tools.desc: The functions a symbol calls.

tools.name: codeweb_impact
tools.desc: Blast radius: every function transitively affected by a change, plus domains touched. Call before editing.

tools.name: codeweb_cycles
tools.desc: File-level dependency cycles (circular imports/calls).

tools.name: codeweb_orphans
tools.desc: Uncalled, unexported symbols — dead-code candidates.

toolPhases.title: Write-time intelligence
toolPhases.blurb: Before you add code — reuse, don't reinvent.

tools.name: codeweb_find_similar
tools.desc: "Does something already do this?" Ranks existing bodies by token-shingle or structural-skeleton similarity to your candidate.

tools.name: codeweb_placement
tools.desc: Where a new symbol belongs (domain + file) by callee gravity, with a duplicate warning.

tools.name: codeweb_tests
tools.desc: The tests that exercise a symbol — run the right subset after editing.

tools.name: codeweb_review
tools.desc: Structural review of a change: changed symbols, blast radius, domains touched, fan-in-ranked review order.

tools.name: codeweb_fitness
tools.desc: Check the graph against architectural fitness rules (forbidden-dependency, layering, no-cycles, max-fan-in, max-LOC).

tools.name: codeweb_risk
tools.desc: Rank symbols by change-risk (fan-in, fan-out, LOC, blast radius, churn) for triage.

tools.name: codeweb_break_cycles
tools.desc: For each file cycle, propose the cheapest edge to sever — verified to actually break it.

tools.name: codeweb_deadcode
tools.desc: Confidence-tiered dead code: safe-to-delete vs review-first (test-guarded / entrypoint-like).

tools.name: codeweb_codemod
tools.desc: Plan a consolidation merge (report-only over MCP): canonical survivor, exact deletions + caller rewrites, LOC reclaimed, projected gate verdict.

tools.name: codeweb_hotspots
tools.desc: Rank symbols by complexity × fan-in × churn — where to refactor first.

tools.name: codeweb_campaign
tools.desc: An ordered, cumulatively pre-flighted ROI worklist (dead code + cycle cuts + merges). Read-only plan.

tools.name: codeweb_context
tools.desc: Blast-radius-scoped context in one call: a symbol's body, direct callers (with bodies), callees (location-only), and transitive impact ids.

tools.name: codeweb_refresh
tools.desc: Re-extract from disk so mid-task queries reflect edits, not a stale snapshot. Incremental; preserves domains.

tools.name: codeweb_diff
tools.desc: Structural delta + regression verdict between two graph snapshots: nodes/edges/cycles/overlaps/orphans added & removed, coupling delta.

tools.name: codeweb_reading_order
tools.desc: Foundations-first reading path (depended-upon leaves before orchestrators) for onboarding at scale.

features.name: Scoped context
features.desc: codeweb_context — a bounded edit window instead of grepping whole files.

features.name: Live refresh
features.desc: codeweb_refresh — incremental re-extract so queries reflect edits.

features.name: Duplication-delta gate
features.desc: review --before — fail the gate when a changed symbol becomes a body-confirmed duplicate.

features.name: Hotspots
features.desc: complexity × fan-in × churn — where to focus first.

features.name: Campaign
features.desc: One ordered, gated worklist of dead-code deletes, cycle cuts, and merges.

features.name: Structural clones
features.desc: Catch renamed (Type-2) clones via an identifier-normalized skeleton.

features.name: Suppression memory
features.desc: Fingerprinted false-positive suppression that a genuinely new issue can't hide behind.

features.name: Incremental edges
features.desc: Per-file edge cache — byte-identical to a full extract, re-derived only for changed files.

features.name: Sharded subgraphs
features.desc: Answer queries from one shard + a boundary index, same result as the monolith.

claims.metric: 1 distinct digest per repo across 20 runs, all 6 repos

claims.claim: Cycles, impact, callers, callees, context match independent oracles
Edit-safety pre-flight predicts the real gate verdict

claims.metric: full pipeline runs on empty node_modules

claims.claim: Agent edit-quality on clean, well-scoped tasks
claims.metric: paired difference = 0 (floor effect, not a power result)

dontClaim.we_say: High-precision, body-confirmed overlap on the clones it surfaces (F1 1.0 synthetic, 0.98 on real axios) — it deliberately drops ambiguous edges because a false positive costs more than a miss.
It produces candidate lists and shows which merges are cycle-safe to apply — a structural pre-flight, not a semantic verdict.
It improves caller-discovery recall (+0.27) and cuts discovery steps (~34%) on frontier agents. The full edit-quality A/B (H18) was a null on clean tasks — a floor effect, reported honestly.
Byte-deterministic, set-valued output — one digest across 20 runs per repo for nodes, edges, domains, and overlaps.
Typical query ~95–120 ms; worst-case p95 264 ms on 3,201 symbols, where Node startup (~63 ms) dominates.
We compare only to stated, reproducible baselines (name-matching, lexical, random, the legacy fallback) — no head-to-head against tools we can't reproduce.
